preview.summary: Ghana and Panama meet in a pivotal Group L opener at BMO Field, with both sides knowing points are precious in a group also featuring England and Croatia. Ghana lean on midfield power and pace despite a key injury blow, while Panama bring an unbeaten qualifying run and a disciplined, experienced spine. A scrappy, tightly-contested affair is likely.
preview.homeForm: Qualified for their fifth World Cup under Carlos Queiroz, ranked in the lower reaches of the field.
preview.awayForm: Ranked around 33rd and qualified unbeaten through CONCACAF under Thomas Christiansen.
preview.homeKey: Thomas Partey, the engine anchoring Ghana's midfield
preview.awayKey: Anibal Godoy, the record-capped captain marshalling the press
storylines: Ghana without star Mohammed Kudus, ruled out by injury
Panama chasing a first-ever World Cup knockout berth
Both sides eyeing a fast start before facing England and Croatia
preview.h2h: The teams have met only sparingly, with no significant competitive history between them.
